_note: Canonical workout type and quality definitions for CoachME. Used by all screens for tooltips and labels. Will mirror the backend codebase definition when development begins.

E.label: Easy
E.definition: A conversational-pace run that builds aerobic base and promotes recovery. You should be able to speak in full sentences comfortably throughout.

MQ.label: Moderate Quality
MQ.definition: A comfortably hard effort incorporating moderate-intensity work such as fartlek segments or marathon-pace miles. Harder than easy, less demanding than a full quality session.

HQ.label: High Quality
HQ.definition: A challenging session targeting your lactate threshold or aerobic capacity — includes intervals, tempo work, or race-pace efforts. Requires full recovery before the next hard day.

LR.label: Long Run
LR.definition: Your weekly endurance cornerstone at easy to moderate pace, building the aerobic base and mental toughness needed for race day.
LR.note: LR is both a quality and a type.

R.label: Rest
R.definition: A scheduled recovery day. Adaptation and fitness gains happen during rest — this is a critical part of your training, not an absence of it.

XT.label: Cross Train
XT.definition: Non-running aerobic activity (cycling, swimming, elliptical) that maintains cardiovascular fitness while reducing impact stress on your legs.

SSR.label: Steady State Run
SSR.definition: A sustained effort at comfortably hard pace — below race pace but harder than easy. Builds lactate threshold and improves running economy. You should be able to speak only in short phrases.

LR.label: Long Run
LR.definition: Your weekly endurance cornerstone at easy to moderate pace, building the aerobic base and mental toughness needed for race day.

R.label: Rest
R.definition: A scheduled recovery day. No running. Essential for adaptation and injury prevention.

XT.label: Cross Train
XT.definition: Non-running aerobic activity that maintains fitness while reducing impact load.

open_questions: Can a Steady State Run be prescribed at MQ quality, or is it always HQ?
Are there additional workout types to be added (e.g. Intervals, Race, Recovery Run)?
Day Screen stats (Last 7 days / Week to date): should they be anchored to today always, or shift when viewing a past/future day? Currently anchored to today — flagged for product decision.
